methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mazama is a Series A–stage climate and energy company founded by Sriram Vasantharajan that has raised $30M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage private companies (Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) valued between $110M and $200M, supplemented by a broader comparison universe of eight private firms and one public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $30M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, implying a $60M valuation. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of the three same-stage, same-sector comparables at $150M. These two approaches yield materially different outputs, reflecting the sparse data on Mazama's specific metrics relative to peers.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $51M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $105M across the two methods. The wide dispersion—spanning 3.4x from low to high—reflects meaningful uncertainty. Key drivers include whether Mazama's capital efficiency and growth trajectory align more closely with the lower-funded stage-progression benchmark or the higher peer medians, and limited visibility into unit economics or revenue traction relative to named comparables. Resolution of these gaps would narrow the range considerably.
